meaning: adj.鲁莽的,顽固的,任性的,性急的,猛烈的
examples: After the heady days that led it to power amid the protests of the so-called Tulip Revolution of 2005, the government is now under pressure to show results.
The heady aroma of wood smoke emanated from the stove.
These are heady times for social media visionaries who have created a wave of viral excitement.
Heady profits have led to a global capital-investment binge, most easily seen in Asia.
